title: Gold EA just did exactly what it was built for 😤 (and yeah I used to overthink entries like a dummy)
author: Loose-Object-8913
selftext:
Quick tester clip from my gold EA 🔥

This is the exact kinda move it’s built for — strong trend, those little pullbacks, then it just keeps adding positions as price pushes higher. No forcing anything.

I used to obsess over the “perfect” entry like it was gonna make or break me… honestly didn’t move the needle at all lol.

What actually changed the game was how the trades are managed after entry. In this clip you can see it adding into the move, but every position is sized properly so it’s never just blind risk stacking. Took me FOREVER to get that part right tbh (still tweaking it sometimes).

Right now live it’s actually sitting out — no clean setup so it’s just chilling.

Curious what you guys think of this style on gold? Single clean entries or building into the trend like this? Drop your thoughts 👇

MQL5- https://www.mql5.com/en/market/product/168039

YouTube- https://youtu.be/Q0jbBDaYQaY?si=oshnI-fdSNSwnyjI

comments.author: Loose-Object-8913
comments.body:
Just to clear it up — the EA only adds on those clean pullbacks with strict position sizing (nothing crazy like martingale, I learned that the hard way last year lol).
Right now live it’s still sitting out, no setup = no trade.
Anyone else scaling into gold trends like this or you guys strictly one-and-done entries? Drop your setup below, curious af

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body: yeah gold trends are nice when they actually trend lol, that’s when EAs look like geniuses. just be careful when it chops sideways for 3 days straight, that’s where most of mine got wrecked. scaling in works great… until it doesn’t.

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body: Yea this is what I was keeping in mind because when gold ranges it really does range so my EA has filters so it sits on the side lines until gold shows a regime change. Risk control around these times was my main goal.

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body: lol gold trends look amazing in tester clips tbh. just make sure it’s not one of those setups that crushes in backtests and then chops you up in live when gold goes sideways for a week. but yeah managing adds > perfect entries imo.

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body:
yeah that’s exactly what I was trying to avoid tbh

gold looks amazing in trends then just goes dead sideways and ruins everything if you’re not careful

that’s why I ended up focusing more on when not to trade rather than trying to improve entries

it’ll just sit out if conditions look messy instead of forcing it

still testing that part the most though, because that’s where most systems fall apart
